title: How to Get a False Criminal Case Quashed in Bangladesh
metaTitle: How to Quash a False Criminal Case in Bangladesh – Legal Guide
metaDescription: Facing a false FIR or fabricated criminal case in Bangladesh? Learn how to get a case quashed in the High Court Division — grounds, process, timeline, and costs.
heroIntro: Fabricated criminal cases are used as tools of harassment in Bangladesh — often arising from family disputes, business rivalries, or political vendettas. The High Court Division has the power to quash (cancel) an FIR or criminal proceeding where the case is manifestly false or an abuse of process.
toc: Where to File: High Court Division
The Quashing Process Step by Step

sections.content: Quashing is a High Court order cancelling a criminal proceeding — typically an FIR, charge sheet, or the entire case. Once quashed, the case is legally dead — no arrest can be made on that FIR, no trial can proceed, and acquittal is not even required. A criminal lawyer files a writ petition or criminal miscellaneous case in the High Court Division under its inherent powers (Section 561A CrPC) or under Article 102 of the Constitution.

The High Court will quash a case on these grounds:

  • The allegations in the FIR, even if taken at face value, do not constitute any criminal offence
  • The FIR is manifestly attended with mala fide intent (filed for harassment, not for seeking justice)
  • The case is barred by limitation
  • There is no legal evidence — the case rests entirely on interested witnesses' bare statements
  • The matter is purely civil in nature dressed up as a criminal complaint
  • Continued prosecution would be an abuse of the court process
  • The parties have reached a genuine compromise (especially in compoundable offences)

sections.h2: The Quashing Process Step by Step
sections.content:
1. Engage a Supreme Court/criminal lawyer to analyse the FIR and build the quashing application.
  2. File a Criminal Miscellaneous Case (Crl. Misc.) in the High Court Division under Section 561A CrPC, supported by an affidavit and relevant documents.
  3. The court may issue a Rule Nisi (show cause) and a Stay of Proceedings — pausing the trial court case while the High Court considers the matter.
  4. The State (government) files an affidavit-in-opposition.
  5. Both sides are heard and the High Court passes judgment — either making the Rule absolute (quashing the case) or discharging it.

Contact a Supreme Court lawyer in Bangladesh immediately if you are facing a false case.

One of the key strategic advantages of filing a quashing petition is securing a Stay of Proceedings order that pauses the trial court case below. This prevents the complainant from getting an arrest warrant or pushing the case to trial while you wait for the High Court ruling. Your lawyer will apply for interim stay simultaneously with the quashing application.

faqs.question: How long does a quashing case take in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: Cases where a stay is granted can take 6 months to 2 years to be fully decided. Urgent cases with clear merit may be resolved faster. The interim stay protects you during this period.

faqs.question: Can a case be quashed at the trial court level?
faqs.answer: No. Quashing jurisdiction lies exclusively with the High Court Division. Trial courts can discharge accused, but cannot quash an FIR.

faqs.question: What is the difference between discharge and quashing?
faqs.answer: Discharge happens at trial court level after charge framing — the accused is released. Quashing happens in the High Court and kills the proceeding before trial even begins. Quashing is more comprehensive.

faqs.question: Can a non-compoundable offence be quashed?
faqs.answer: Generally no, but the High Court has discretion where it finds the case to be an abuse of process, even for serious offences. Each case depends on its specific facts.
